title: pyrola asarifolia var. bracteata
intro: Pyrola asarifolia var. bracteata, also known as the western wintergreen or bracted wintergreen, is a plant species belonging to the heath family, Ericaceae. It is a low-growing, evergreen perennial with oval-shaped leaves and white flowers that are highly fragrant and attract pollinators. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Pyrola asarifolia var. bracteata include its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, and it can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for medicinal use.
